mnemonics: ANOVA's Core: A Variance Officer Named Pearson Always Analyzes Variation
keyPoints: ANOVA (Analysis of Variance) is a statistical method used to compare the means of three or more groups.
It determines if the differences between group means are statistically significant or due to random chance.
It partitions the total observed variance into components attributable to different sources (e.g., between-group variance and within-group variance).
commonMistakes: Assuming ANOVA can be used to compare only two groups (use a t-test instead).
Applying ANOVA without checking its assumptions (normality, homogeneity of variances, independence).
Concluding *which* specific groups are different after a significant ANOVA result without performing a post-hoc test.
